growth_records.medium: Wolfe's medium (Swarup et al. 2014) with 10 g/L D-glucose

up.method: biomass-specific consumption rate, exponential phase (HPLC HPX-87H)

sec.method: biomass-specific production rate, exponential phase; sole byproduct

growth_records.cite: Ljungqvist, Emil et al. Insights into the rapid metabolism of Geobacillus sp. LC300: unraveling metabolic requirements and optimal growth conditions. Extremophiles (2023). DOI:10.1007/s00792-023-01319-x. [PMC10689506]
growth_records.snippet: In Wolfe's medium, the cells displayed exponential growth with a growth rate of 1.56 (± 0.10) h−1 ... glucose consumption rate ... (16.1 ± 0.70 mmol gDW−1 h−1) ... acetate production rates of ... 7.11 (± 0.84) mmol gDW−1 h−1 in Wolfe's medi
growth_records.notes: Bioreactor batch, 68 C, Wolfe's medium. mu=1.56±0.10/h reported. doubling_time=ln2/mu=0.693147/1.56=0.444 h. glucose qS=16.1±0.70 mmol/gDW/h and acetate qP=7.11±0.84 mmol/gDW/h both reported directly (exponential phase). Starting pH set with 4M H2SO4 but exact value not given for this run (Tris buffer nominal pH 8); pH left null.

growth_records.medium: Defined minimal medium supplemented with full Wolfe's vitamins, 10 g/L glucose

sec.method: biomass-specific production rate, exponential phase; sole byproduct

growth_records.cite: Ljungqvist, Emil et al. Insights into the rapid metabolism of Geobacillus sp. LC300: unraveling metabolic requirements and optimal growth conditions. Extremophiles (2023). DOI:10.1007/s00792-023-01319-x. [PMC10689506]
growth_records.snippet: in the defined medium, exponential growth was sustained with a growth rate of 1.70 (± 0.09) h−1 until 2 gDW L−1 ... glucose consumption rate in the exponential phase was higher in the defined medium (25.5 ± 1.26 mmol gDW−1 h−1) ... acetate
growth_records.notes: Bioreactor batch, 68 C, defined medium + full Wolfe's vitamin mix (Fig.1). mu=1.70±0.09/h reported; doubling=ln2/1.70=0.408 h. glucose qS=25.5±1.26, acetate qP=9.94±0.97 mmol/gDW/h reported directly. Salt composition taken from final MLGM (Table 2); this earlier defined-medium run predates the NTA/Mg-reduction tweak that defines MLGM and used full Wolfe's vitamins rather than biotin+B12 only. pH not stated for this run.

growth_records.medium: Defined minimal medium supplemented with biotin + vitamin B12 only, 10 g/L glucose

sec.method: biomass-specific production rate, exponential phase; sole byproduct

growth_records.cite: Ljungqvist, Emil et al. Insights into the rapid metabolism of Geobacillus sp. LC300: unraveling metabolic requirements and optimal growth conditions. Extremophiles (2023). DOI:10.1007/s00792-023-01319-x. [PMC10689506]
growth_records.snippet: supplementation with only biotin and vitamin B12 resulted in a maximum biomass specific growth rate of 1.76 (± 0.05) h−1 ... specific acetate production rate of 8.9 (± 0.2) mmol gDW−1 h−1, and glucose was consumed with a specific rate of 25
growth_records.notes: Bioreactor batch, 68 C, defined medium + biotin(0.4uM)+B12(3.7nM) only (Fig.3). mu=1.76±0.05/h reported; doubling=ln2/1.76=0.394 h. glucose qS=25.8±1.1, acetate qP=8.9±0.2 mmol/gDW/h reported directly. Salts per Table 2 MLGM base (NTA not necessarily present in this run). pH not stated for this run.

growth_records.medium: Minimal LC300 growth medium (MLGM), 10 g/L glucose

sec.method: maximum biomass-specific production rate; sole byproduct before O2 limitation

growth_records.cite: Ljungqvist, Emil et al. Insights into the rapid metabolism of Geobacillus sp. LC300: unraveling metabolic requirements and optimal growth conditions. Extremophiles (2023). DOI:10.1007/s00792-023-01319-x. [PMC10689506]
growth_records.snippet: the maximum biomass specific growth rate was 2.20 (± 0.04) h−1 ... biomass yield during this fully aerobic phase was 0.35 gDW gglc−1 ... overall biomass yield on glucose of 0.30 gDW gglc−1. The glucose was consumed with a maximum biomass sp
growth_records.notes: Bioreactor batch on final MLGM (Table 2), confirmed-optimal 65 C, pH 6.75 (Fig.6). Fastest reported defined-medium growth. mu=2.20±0.04/h; doubling=ln2/2.20=0.315 h. glucose qS=35.7±1.1, acetate qP=10.1±0.56 mmol/gDW/h reported. Yxs 0.30 (overall) and 0.35 (aerobic phase) gDW/g_glucose reported; final cell mass ~3.5 g/L.

growth_records.medium: MLGM (defined minimal) with xylose as sole carbon source
growth_records.cite: Ljungqvist, Emil et al. Insights into the rapid metabolism of Geobacillus sp. LC300: unraveling metabolic requirements and optimal growth conditions. Extremophiles (2023). DOI:10.1007/s00792-023-01319-x. [PMC10689506]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1 Achieved growth rates ... Xylose 1.25 (± 0.01) ... Values are mean of triplicate shake flask cultivations, ± standard deviation.
growth_records.notes: Shake-flask screen (Table 1), MLGM at optimal 65 C/pH6.75, xylose sole C source. mu_max=1.25±0.01/h reported; doubling=ln2/1.25=0.554 h. No uptake/secretion rates measured in the screen.

growth_records.medium: MLGM (defined minimal) with galactose as sole carbon source
growth_records.cite: Ljungqvist, Emil et al. Insights into the rapid metabolism of Geobacillus sp. LC300: unraveling metabolic requirements and optimal growth conditions. Extremophiles (2023). DOI:10.1007/s00792-023-01319-x. [PMC10689506]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1 ... Galactose 0.72 (± 0.03) ... triplicate shake flask cultivations
growth_records.notes: Table 1 screen, MLGM, galactose sole C source. mu_max=0.72±0.03/h; doubling=ln2/0.72=0.963 h.

growth_records.medium: MLGM (defined minimal) with maltose as sole carbon source
growth_records.cite: Ljungqvist, Emil et al. Insights into the rapid metabolism of Geobacillus sp. LC300: unraveling metabolic requirements and optimal growth conditions. Extremophiles (2023). DOI:10.1007/s00792-023-01319-x. [PMC10689506]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1 ... Maltose 0.76 (± 0.01) ... triplicate shake flask cultivations
growth_records.notes: Table 1 screen, MLGM, maltose sole C source. mu_max=0.76±0.01/h (lowest among growth-supporting sources); doubling=ln2/0.76=0.912 h.

growth_records.medium: MLGM (defined minimal) with sucrose as sole carbon source
growth_records.cite: Ljungqvist, Emil et al. Insights into the rapid metabolism of Geobacillus sp. LC300: unraveling metabolic requirements and optimal growth conditions. Extremophiles (2023). DOI:10.1007/s00792-023-01319-x. [PMC10689506]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1 ... Sucrose 1.91 (± 0.01) ... triplicate shake flask cultivations
growth_records.notes: Table 1 screen, MLGM, sucrose sole C source. mu_max=1.91±0.01/h; doubling=ln2/1.91=0.363 h.

growth_records.cite: Ljungqvist, Emil et al. Insights into the rapid metabolism of Geobacillus sp. LC300: unraveling metabolic requirements and optimal growth conditions. Extremophiles (2023). DOI:10.1007/s00792-023-01319-x. [PMC10689506]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1 ... Cellobiose 1.89 (± 0.05) ... triplicate shake flask cultivations
growth_records.notes: Table 1 screen, MLGM, cellobiose sole C source. mu_max=1.89±0.05/h; doubling=ln2/1.89=0.367 h.

growth_records.medium: MLGM (defined minimal) with soluble starch as sole carbon source
growth_records.cite: Ljungqvist, Emil et al. Insights into the rapid metabolism of Geobacillus sp. LC300: unraveling metabolic requirements and optimal growth conditions. Extremophiles (2023). DOI:10.1007/s00792-023-01319-x. [PMC10689506]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1 ... Starch 1.42 (± 0.06) ... triplicate shake flask cultivations
growth_records.notes: Table 1 screen, MLGM, starch sole C source. mu_max=1.42±0.06/h; doubling=ln2/1.42=0.488 h.

growth_records.medium: MLGM (defined minimal) with mannitol as sole carbon source
growth_records.cite: Ljungqvist, Emil et al. Insights into the rapid metabolism of Geobacillus sp. LC300: unraveling metabolic requirements and optimal growth conditions. Extremophiles (2023). DOI:10.1007/s00792-023-01319-x. [PMC10689506]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1 ... Mannitol 1.56 (± 0.04) ... triplicate shake flask cultivations
growth_records.notes: Table 1 screen, MLGM, mannitol sole C source. mu_max=1.56±0.04/h; doubling=ln2/1.56=0.444 h.

growth_records.medium: MLGM (defined minimal) with glycerol as sole carbon source
growth_records.cite: Ljungqvist, Emil et al. Insights into the rapid metabolism of Geobacillus sp. LC300: unraveling metabolic requirements and optimal growth conditions. Extremophiles (2023). DOI:10.1007/s00792-023-01319-x. [PMC10689506]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1 ... Glycerol 1.95 (± 0.002) ... Glycerol data were collected from duplicate bioreactor cultivations
growth_records.notes: Table 1, glycerol sole C source, duplicate BIOREACTOR (not shake flask) cultivations at 65 C/pH6.75. mu_max=1.95±0.002/h; doubling=ln2/1.95=0.355 h.

growth_records.medium: MLGM (defined minimal) with acetate as sole carbon source
growth_records.cite: Ljungqvist, Emil et al. Insights into the rapid metabolism of Geobacillus sp. LC300: unraveling metabolic requirements and optimal growth conditions. Extremophiles (2023). DOI:10.1007/s00792-023-01319-x. [PMC10689506]
growth_records.snippet: Of the organic acids tested, growth was only supported on acetate (0.69 h−1). ... Table 1 ... Acetate 0.69 (± 0.02)
growth_records.notes: Table 1 screen, MLGM, acetate sole C source (here consumed as substrate, not a byproduct). mu_max=0.69±0.02/h; doubling=ln2/0.69=1.005 h. Citrate/rhamnose/arabinose/fructose/lactose gave no growth.

growth_records.medium: Defined LC300 medium (Swarup et al. 2014) with D-glucose

up.method: HPLC (Aminex HPX-87H), max biomass-specific rate in log phase, mean of quadruplicate ±0.70
off-gas analyzer, mean of duplicate ±3.85 mean deviation

sec.method: HPLC, max biomass-specific rate in log phase, mean of quadruplicate ±0.84; sole by-product on glucose
off-gas analyzer, mean of duplicate ±0.33 mean deviation

yields.note: Carbon balance gap 17.8% and degree-of-reduction gap 6.1% on glucose

growth_records.cite: Ljungqvist, Emil et al. Genome-scale reconstruction and metabolic modelling of the fast-growing thermophile Geobacillus sp. LC300. Metabolic Engineering Communications (2022). DOI:10.1016/j.mec.2022.e00212. [PMC9678985]
growth_records.snippet: Glucose μ 1.56 (±0.10) h-1; Substrate 16.05 (±0.70); Acetate 7.11 (±0.84); Oxygen 34.41 (±3.85); Carbon dioxide 24.33 (±0.33) mmol gDW-1 h-1 (Table 2)
growth_records.notes: All rates are experimentally measured max biomass-specific consumption/production rates during logarithmic phase (Table 2). mu=1.56 h-1 reported. doubling_time computed td=ln2/mu=0.693/1.56=0.444 h (26.7 min). CDW from OD600 via measured ratio 1.9. Model iGEL604 PREDICTED mu=1.51 h-1 (model, not experimental). 13C-MFA validation used external data of Cordova & Antoniewicz 2016 / Cordova et al. 2017 (grown at 72C), NOT measured in this paper. Acetate was the sole by-product in log phase on glucose.

growth_records.medium: Defined LC300 medium (Swarup et al. 2014) with D-xylose

up.method: HPLC (Aminex HPX-87H), max biomass-specific rate in log phase, mean of quadruplicate ±0.60
off-gas analyzer, mean of duplicate ±1.21 mean deviation

sec.method: off-gas analyzer, mean of duplicate ±2.82 mean deviation

yields.note: Carbon balance gap 12.3% and degree-of-reduction gap 9.7% on xylose

growth_records.cite: Ljungqvist, Emil et al. Genome-scale reconstruction and metabolic modelling of the fast-growing thermophile Geobacillus sp. LC300. Metabolic Engineering Communications (2022). DOI:10.1016/j.mec.2022.e00212. [PMC9678985]
growth_records.snippet: Xylose μ 1.07 (±0.06) h-1; Substrate 13.61 (±0.60); Acetate –; Oxygen 32.74 (±1.21); Carbon dioxide 31.81 (±2.82) mmol gDW-1 h-1 (Table 2)
growth_records.notes: Experimentally measured max biomass-specific rates during log phase (Table 2). mu=1.07 h-1 reported. doubling td=ln2/mu=0.693/1.07=0.648 h (38.9 min). No acetate (or other by-products) detected during log phase on xylose (Table 2 shows '–'); acetate was produced only in early stationary phase (not quantified as a log-phase rate). Model iGEL604 PREDICTED mu=1.00 h-1 (model, not experimental).

yield_fit.note: qS = (1/Yxs)·µ + m_s  — linear fit of carbon-source uptake vs growth rate
